title: Transfer Switch Events
exclusions: Does not certify NEC separation distances, substitute arc-flash engineering studies, or replace vendor ATS commissioning reports—only correlates telemetry already modeled as sourcetype "ats:events".
evidence: Retain exported alert results showing _time, ats_id, event, source_side, duration_ms plus screenshots or SNMP MIB polls proving feeder state during the window for retroactive audits.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Operators simulate a mains dropout on ATS-GSS1 so SNMP normalization emits transfer_to_gen with source_side moving from utility to generator and duration_ms recorded between 120 ms and 450 ms; within five minutes the scheduled search returns exactly one qualifying row per ats_id and the alert triggers.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Network maintenance bounces the SNMP collector interface without any ATS transfer; traps contain coldStart linkUp noise lacking event and duration_ms fields—after ingestion those rows fail the SPL predicates and the alert stays idle.

dataSources: Land ATS telemetry on index=power as sourcetype="ats:events" with Splunk-extracted dimensions ats_id, event (for example transfer_to_gen, retransfer_to_utility, test), source_side, duration_ms, and the relay or trap source field referenced in props. Typical collection paths are SNMP traps or polled OIDs from the ATS SNMP/BMS integration described in App, dry-contact closures into a syslog or HEC bridge, Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase app ID 6266), or SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase app ID 1537) on a Heavy Forwarder with UDP/162 trap stanzas in inputs.conf.
spl:
index=power sourcetype="ats:events"
| where event IN ("transfer_to_gen","retransfer_to_utility","test") OR duration_ms > 500
| table _time, ats_id, event, source_side, duration_ms
| sort -_time
description: This use case tracks automatic transfer switch activity normalized to sourcetype "ats:events": utility-to-generator transfers, generator retransfers to utility, operator-initiated exercises, and dwell-time anomalies reflected in duration_ms and source_side.
value: Operations and facilities teams gain a time-ordered narrative of mains versus emergency feed changes, which tightens root-cause analysis after blips, proves whether loads actually rode through, and prevents repeat equipment stress from undiagnosed hunting or slow transfer sequences.
implementation: Normalize ATS SNMP/BMS or dry-contact relay signals into ats:events, keep management-interface clocks aligned to NTP, run the catalog SPL on index=power with tunable duration_ms thresholds, and correlate hits with upstream utility or generator maintenance windows before opening an incident.
visualization: Primary: Timeline colored by event across each ats_id for shift reviews. Secondary: Table sorted by latest transfer per ATS with sparkline-ready duration_ms. KPI: Single-value panel counting qualifying transitions over `-24h` with drill-down to raw events.
knownFalsePositives: Scenario A — Quarterly ATS exercise programmes emit event=test bursts from the ATS SNMP/BMS stream; distinguish them with a maintenance_calendar lookup keyed on site_code and suppress paging outside drill windows while still indexing the rows. Scenario B — Relay chatter from adjacent HVAC/BMS panels shares the forwarding host and mimics transitions; require populated ats_id plus sourcetype="ats:events" before firing. Scenario C — SNMP agent warm reboots replay stale transfer traps with duplicate _time skew under one second; collapse duplicates with dedup ats_id event within a two-second span. Scenario D — Vendor firmware emits prolonged duration_ms spikes during benign capacitor bank switching; tune thresholds per ATS model via a thresholds.csv lookup joined on ats_id.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Deploy collection that matches App: ATS SNMP/BMS traps/polls plus optional dry-contact relays—prefer Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase app ID 6266) or SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase app ID 1537) on a Heavy Forwarder that owns UDP/162 trap listeners declared in inputs.conf, forwarding into index=power with sourcetype="ats:events".
• Validate props/transforms extract ats_id, event, source_side, duration_ms, and trap identity fields before alerting; spot-check Interactive Search over `-24h@h` for field completeness.
• RBAC: grant facility engineers a scoped role such as power_facility_ops read/search on index=power only; dedicate a scheduler account with schedule_search for the saved detection.
• Document ATS-specific enumerated event tokens your normalization emits so Splunk admins map trap OID payloads consistently.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map SNMP trap bindings or relay closures into structured rows with `_time` anchored to device-reported timestamps; if relays arrive via syslog, tune LINE_BREAKER/TIME_PREFIX so `_time` reflects switch action, not forwarder receipt lag.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the UC SPL into Search, verify rows for known transfers and drills, then save as an alert with cron_schedule `*/5 * * * *` paired to dispatch.earliest_time `-5m` so oscillations surface quickly without overlapping windows.

Step 3 — Validate
Cross-check Splunk rows against the ATS local status indication or an SNMP poll/walk from the same management VLAN for the same ats_id during the `_time` window; reconcile discrepancies before promoting to production schedules.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Publish the query on an operational dashboard (timeline plus sortable table), route alert actions through your paging policy, record tuning thresholds for duration_ms and allowed event vocabulary in the facility runbook, and rehearse DR drills with suppression macros keyed on maintenance CSV lookups.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
